NEC 110.14.title: Electrical Connections
NEC 110.14.requirement: Terminals for more than one conductor must be identified. Conductors must be compatible with terminal material.

NEC 210.8.title: GFCI Protection
NEC 210.8.requirement: GFCI protection required for 125V through 250V receptacles in bathrooms, kitchens, garages, outdoors, crawl spaces, unfinished basements, laundry areas, and within 6 ft of sinks.

NEC 210.11.title: Branch Circuit Requirements
NEC 210.11.requirement: Minimum branch circuits: 2 small-appliance circuits (20A) for kitchen/dining, 1 bathroom circuit (20A), 1 laundry circuit (20A).

NEC 210.12.title: AFCI Protection
NEC 210.12.requirement: Arc-fault circuit-interrupter protection required for 120V, 15A and 20A branch circuits supplying outlets in dwelling unit bedrooms, living rooms, dining rooms, family rooms, closets, hallways, sunrooms.

NEC 210.52.title: Dwelling Unit Receptacle Outlets
NEC 210.52.requirement: Receptacles required so no point along floor line in any wall space is more than 6 ft from an outlet. Kitchen countertop: every 4 ft, GFCI protected. Island countertops 12 inches or wider need at least one outlet.

NEC 220.12.title: General Lighting Loads
NEC 220.12.requirement: General lighting load: 3 VA per sq ft for dwellings, 3.5 VA/sqft for office, 2 VA/sqft for warehouse.

NEC 220.82.title: Optional Dwelling Load Calculation
NEC 220.82.requirement: First 10 kVA of general loads at 100%, remainder at 40%. Add largest motor at 125%. AC/heat at largest value.

NEC 230.42.title: Service-Entrance Conductor Sizing
NEC 230.42.requirement: Service-entrance conductors must have ampacity not less than the maximum load per Article 220. Minimum 100A for single dwelling.

NEC 230.70.title: Service Disconnect Location
NEC 230.70.requirement: Service disconnecting means must be installed at a readily accessible location nearest the point of entrance of the service conductors.

NEC 230.79.title: Service Disconnect Rating
NEC 230.79.requirement: Minimum 100A for single dwelling. Must be rated for the calculated load per Article 220.

NEC 240.4.title: Conductor Overcurrent Protection
NEC 240.4.requirement: Conductors must be protected against overcurrent per their ampacity. #14 AWG max 15A, #12 AWG max 20A, #10 AWG max 30A.

NEC 250.66.title: Grounding Electrode Conductor Sizing
NEC 250.66.requirement: GEC sized per Table 250.66. For 1/0 AWG copper service: #6 AWG copper GEC. For 3/0 or larger: #4 AWG copper GEC.

NEC 300.5.title: Underground Installation Burial Depth
NEC 300.5.requirement: Direct burial cable: 24 inches. Rigid metal conduit: 6 inches. PVC conduit: 18 inches. Under dwelling driveway: 18 inches for RMC.

NEC 334.80.title: NM Cable (Romex) Ampacity
NEC 334.80.requirement: NM cable ampacity based on 60C column of Table 310.16. Maximum 3 current-carrying conductors without derating.

NEC 344.30.title: Rigid Metal Conduit Support
NEC 344.30.requirement: RMC must be supported within 3 ft of each box/enclosure and at intervals not exceeding 10 ft.

NEC 348.22.title: Flexible Metal Conduit Fill
NEC 348.22.requirement: Maximum conductor fill: 53% for 1 conductor, 31% for 2 conductors, 40% for 3+ conductors (same as Chapter 9 Table 1).

NEC 358.30.title: EMT Support and Securing
NEC 358.30.requirement: EMT must be supported within 3 ft of each box and every 10 ft. For conduit larger than 1-1/4 inch: straps within 3 ft, every 10 ft.

NEC 422.11.title: Appliance Overcurrent Protection
NEC 422.11.requirement: Branch circuit rating for individual appliance: not exceed 150% of appliance rated amperage. Continuous load: 125% of rated current.

NEC 430.52.title: Motor Branch Circuit Protection
NEC 430.52.requirement: Motor branch circuit short-circuit protection: dual-element fuse max 175% of motor FLA, inverse-time breaker max 250% of motor FLA.

NEC 680.22.title: Swimming Pool Lighting
NEC 680.22.requirement: Underwater luminaires must operate at 15V or less, or be listed for the purpose with GFCI protection. No junction boxes within 4 ft of pool edge.

conduit_area_sqin.description: Chapter 9 Table 4 — Conduit Internal Area (sq in)

wire_area_sqin.description: Chapter 9 Table 5 — Conductor Area (sq in) THHN/THWN
